commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `plugins.entries.<id>.hooks.allowStateAccess` as a state-only `agent_end` permission, preserves it through config/status/startup surfaces, and updates schema help plus tests and `CHANGELOG.md`.

PR surface: Source +67, Tests +111, Docs +7. Total +185 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the current-state problem: source inspection shows current main classifies `agent_end` as a conversation hook and blocks non-bundled registrations unless `allowConversationAccess` is true. The PR-specific explicit-deny regression is also source-reproducible from the changed registry branch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review and contributor action are needed because this changes a public plugin permission/security boundary and automation cannot supply the required real plugin proof.

target.title: feat(plugins): allowStateAccess permission for agent_end without conversation content
target.body:
Closes #74580

## What Problem This Solves

Non-bundled plugins that only need run-level outcome data from `agent_end` (success/failure, duration, runId) are forced to declare `allowConversationAccess: true` â€” a flag that also grants access to raw conversation content (`llm_input`, `llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`). This is intimidating for users who just want a display to show when the agent is thinking.

PR #78242 by @hclsys attempted to fix this but was closed because `allowStateAccess` was only added to the registry gate â€” the config normalization pipeline in `config-normalization-shared.ts` silently dropped the field before it ever reached runtime policy checks.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add `hooks.allowStateAccess: true` as a granular, additive, non-breaking alternative. When set without `allowConversationAccess`, `agent_end` events are dispatched with `messages: []` â€” conversation content stays protected.

`allowConversationAccess=true` still implies state access, so existing configs are unaffected.

## User Impact

Plugin authors who only need agent lifecycle state (e.g. a display showing processing/sleeping) can now set `allowStateAccess: true` instead of the intimidating `allowConversationAccess: true`. Existing configs with `allowConversationAccess: true` continue working unchanged.

## Changes (17 files, +194/-9)

**Core:**
- `hook-types.ts` â€” `STATE_HOOK_NAMES = ["agent_end"]`, `isStateHookName()` predicate, moved `agent_end` out of `CONVERSATION_HOOK_NAMES`
- `registry.ts` â€” State hook gating: allowed if `allowStateAccess` OR `allowConversationAccess`. When only state access, handler is wrapped to strip `messages` â†’ `[]`
- `config-normalization-shared.ts` â€” **Critical fix for #78242**: `allowStateAccess` preserved through normalization pipeline

**Config & Schema:**
- `zod-schema.ts` â€” `allowStateAccess: z.boolean().optional()`
- `types.plugins.ts` â€” Type + JSDoc
- `schema.help.ts` â€” Public docs for the new field
- `schema.labels.ts` â€” UI label

**Startup & Status:**
- `gateway-startup-plugin-ids.ts` â€” `allowStateAccess` triggers hook-policy startup
- `status.ts` â€” Included in plugin inspect report
- `compat/registry.ts` â€” Surface for compat checks

**Tests:**
- `config-misc.test.ts` â€” Schema acceptance/rejection
- `config-state.test.ts` â€” Normalization preservation + standalone state access
- `channel-plugin-ids.test.ts` â€” Startup intent with only `allowStateAccess`
- `schema.help.quality.test.ts` â€” Help key coverage for `allowStateAccess`
- `loader.test.ts` â€” Updated conversation hook block test (6 hooks), added state hook allow/block tests

**Changelog:** `CHANGELOG.md` â€” Unreleased entry

## Security

- `messages` field in `agent_end` events remains behind `allowConversationAccess` â€” state-only plugins receive `messages: []`
- No new dependencies, workflows, or package scripts
- Additive and non-breaking: `allowConversationAccess=true` implies state access

## Evidence

This is our feature request (#74580) from the OpenClaw Interaction Bridge plugin â€” a physical display that only needs agent lifecycle state. The plugin currently requires `allowConversationAccess: true` just to show sleeping/processing faces. With this change, it can set `allowStateAccess: true` instead.

**Test updates:**
- `loader.test.ts`: Split `agent_end` out of conversation hook block test (6 conversation hooks + 1 state hook with different diagnostic message). Added `allows state hooks when allowStateAccess is enabled` and `blocks state hooks when neither flag is set` tests.
- `schema.help.quality.test.ts`: Assert `agent_end` appears in `allowStateAccess` help text, NOT in `allowConversationAccess` help text.
- `config-state.test.ts`: Normalization preserves `allowStateAccess` through pipeline.
- `channel-plugin-ids.test.ts`: Startup intent recognizes `allowStateAccess`.

CI push ffe5c03 failed `checks-fast-bundled-protocol` due to `loader.test.ts` expecting `agent_end` in conversation hooks (7 blocked â†’ 6 blocked). Fixed in push 15c2d48 with updated test assertions.
